title: Hegseth kills probe into Apache pilots who buzzed Kid Rock's house
author: Séamus Bellamy
summary: Hey, remember in the long, long ago when Jason told you about those Apache attack chopper pilots that were grounded for dropping by Kid Rock's house and not launching Hellfire missiles doing an illegal flyover of his house? Well, they've been
full_text:
Hey, remember in the long, long ago when Jason told you about those Apache attack chopper pilots that were grounded for dropping by Kid Rock's house and not launching Hellfire missiles doing an illegal flyover of his house? Well, they've been let off the hook.

According to The Washington Post, Whisky Pete Hegseth has once again shown that the adults are not in charge. Hours after Army spokesperson Maj. Montrell Russell told the media that the air crews responsible for wasting taxpayer cash on buzzing the home of a waste of concert hall space will not be punished. Further, there would be no investigation into their actions. While this may look like more buffoonery from one of the dumbest monkeys in the whole damn tree, Hegseth's overruling military law has the potential to be so much more dangerous than it appears.

Every member of the U.S. military is an armed, professional killer. Even a soldier who spends their day in a kitchen has been trained to murder folks for the government. Because of this capacity for violence and their access to the tools that allow for it, soldiers, sailors, and airmen are held to a high standard with rules and regulations for damn near everything they do. Terrible things happen in war: so much so that conventions have been set to regulate how armies treat their enemies' captured or wounded soldiers and the civilians caught in the middle of a conflict.

If we can't trust those who oversee the United States military to hold the nation's soldiers to account for very public, banal hijinks like the flyby of a washed-up entertainer's home for a photo-op, how can we believe that crimes committed in the theater of war will be punished or even discouraged?
